items.title: Fed's Beige Book Shows Slight Growth: Consumer Spending Slips As Prices Rise
items.author: Erica Kollmann
items.description: The Federal Reserve's Beige Book showed a slight increase in overall economic activity, but consumer spending decreased while price sensitivity for discretionary items increased, particularly among lower-income consumers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF, which tracks the S&P 500 index, was up 1.17% at the time of publication, indicating a positive market reaction to the Beige Book release.
The Invesco QQQ Trust, which tracks the Nasdaq 100 index, was up 1.43% at the time of publication, also indicating a positive market reaction to the Beige Book release.
